chapter_title.ar: باب أَنَّهُنَّ بِمَنْزِلَةِ الْإِمَاءِ وَ لَيْسَتْ مِنَ الْأَرْبَعِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 94 – They (wives of Mut’a) are at the status of slave girls, and are not from the four (wives allowed)

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (20/231)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (20/231)
text: سُئِلَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) عَنِ الْمُتْعَةِ أَ هِيَ مِنَ الْأَرْبَعِ فَقَالَ لَا وَ لَا مِنَ السَّبْعِينَ .

en.hubeali: Al Husayn Bin Muhammad, from Moalla Bin Muhammad, from Al Hassan Bin Ali, from Hammad Bin Usman, from Abu Baseer who said,
‘Abu Abdullahasws was asked about the Mut’a, is she from the four (allowable wives)?’ So heasws said: ‘No, nor from the seventy (i.e. as many as you so desire to)’.
en.sarwar: 4. Al-Husayn from Muhammad has narrated from Mu’alla’ ibn Muhammad from al-Hassan ibn Ali from Hammad ibn ‘Uthaman from abu Basir who has said the following: “This is concerning my question before abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, if al-Mut‘ah (advantageous marriage) is of four. He (the Imam) said, ‘No, even seventy is not unlawful.’”
